| Reset       | 0x00000     | 0x00000     | 0x00000     | 0x00000     | 0x00000      | 0x00000      | 0x00000      | 0x00000      |
| Access Type | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read  | Write, Read  | Write, Read  | Write, Read  |
| BIT         | 7           | 6           | 5           | 4           | 3            | 2            | 1            | 0            |
| Field       | THIGH[7:0]  | THIGH[7:0]  | THIGH[7:0]  | THIGH[7:0]  | THIGH[7:0]   | THIGH[7:0]   | THIGH[7:0]   | THIGH[7:0]   |

| Access Type | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read  | Write, Read  | Write, Read  | Write, Read  |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| THIGH      | 19:0   | Upper velocity limit for CoolStep/CoolStep+. TSTEP < THIGH disables CoolStep and CoolStep+. Use together with TPWMTRHS for a smooth switch from StealthChop+ with CoolStep+ to Spreadcycle at full current. |

| BITFIELD   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                  |
|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|            | THIGH > TSTEP > TPWMTHRS: CoolStep+ is disabled and current ramps to IRUN , as defined by COOL_PI_OFF_SPEED. |

| BITFIELD          | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|-------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| TSGP_LOW_VEL_THRS | 19:0   | TSTEP- based velocity threshold for low velocity StallGuard+-based stall detection. Set to a lower velocity/higher value than TCOOLTHRS . TSGP_LOW_VEL_THRS > TSTEP > TCOOLTHRS: low velocity StallGuard+ is enabled. |

| Access Type | -                  | -                  | -                  | -                  | Write, Read         | Write, Read         | Write, Read         | Write, Read         |

| BIT         | 7                  | 6                  | 5                  | 4                  | 3                   | 2                   | 1                   | 0                   |
| Field       | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]  | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]  | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]  | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]  | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]   | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]   | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]   | T_RCOIL_MEAS[7:0]   |
| Reset       | 0x1000             | 0x1000             | 0x1000             | 0x1000             | 0x1000              | 0x1000              | 0x1000              | 0x1000              |
| Access Type | Write, Read        | Write, Read        | Write, Read        | Write, Read        | Write, Read         | Write, Read         | Write, Read         | Write, Read         |

| BITFIELD     | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|--------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| T_RCOIL_MEAS | 19:0   | Upper velocity for automatic measurement of coil resistance. TSTEP > T_RCOIL_MEAS : Automatic resistance measurement enabled during low velocity motion or standstill. TSTEP <= T_RCOIL_MEAS : Automatic resistance measurement disabled. |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                  | DECODE                                   |
|------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------|
| udc_enable | 8      | Enable feature. Must be in StealthChop+ mode, Softstop must be activated, TSTEP < TCOOL_THRS , TSTEP > THIGH, D1 , D2, DMAX of rampgen must be set.                          | 0x0: disable udcstep 0x1: enable udcstep |
| ACCEL_THRS | 7:4    | Set < DECEL_THRS. If ( CS_ACTUAL < = (( ACCEL_THRS + 1) × IRUN )/16), increase ramp velocity. In position mode, this might not happen as reaching the target is prioritized. |                                          |
| DECEL_THRS | 3:0    | Set > ACCEL_THRS. If ( CS_ACTUAL >= (( DECEL_THRS + 1) × IRUN )/16), reduce ramp velocity.                                                                                   |                                          |

## RAMPMODE (0x20)

| BIT           | 31   | 30   | 29   | 28   | 27   | 26            | 25   | 24    |
|---------------|------|------|------|------|------|---------------|------|-------|
| Field         | - -  | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             | -    |       |
| -             | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             | -    | Reset |
| Access Type - | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             | -    |       |
| BIT 23        | 22   | 21   | 20   | 19   | 18   | 17            | 16   |       |
| Field -       | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             |      | -     |
| Reset -       | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             | -    |       |
| Access Type - | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             |      | -     |
| BIT 15        | 14   | 13   | 12   | 11   |      | 10            | 9    | 8     |
| Field -       | -    | -    | -    | -    |      | -             | -    | -     |
| Reset -       | -    | -    | -    | -    | -    | -             |      | -     |
| Access Type - | -    | -    | -    | -    |      | -             | -    | -     |
| BIT 7         | 6    | 5    | 4    | 3    |      | 2             | 1    | 0     |
| Field -       | -    | -    | -    |      | - -  | RAMPMODE[1:0] |      |       |
| Reset         | - -  | -    | -    | -    | -    | 0b00          |      |       |
| Access Type   | - -  | -    | -    | -    | -    | Write,        | Read | Read  |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                    | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| RAMPMODE   | 1:0    | Motion Controller ramping mode | 0x0: Positioning mode (using all A, D, and V parameters). 0x1: Velocity mode to positive VMAX (using A1, A2, AMAX, V1, V2, VMAX ). 0x2: Velocity mode to negative VMAX (using A1,A2, AMAX, V1, V2, VMAX). 0x3: Hold mode (velocity remains unchanged, unless stop event occurs). |

|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| TVMAX      | 15:0   | Minimum time for constant velocity segments in multiple of 512 clocks. 0: Disables minimum duration setting for constant velocity phase. >0: A minimum duration of constant velocity is inserted between any change from acceleration to deceleration or vice versa to reduce jerk. 0…(2 16 ) - 1 x 512t CLK Note: Configure this register after setting VMAX when in position mode and standstill. Set TVMAX = 0 during velocity mode to avoid triggering the TVMAX delay when switching back to ramp mode. |

BITS

15:0

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

0x0000

Write, Read DESCRIPTION

Defines the waiting time after ramping down to zero velocity before next movement or

direction inversion can start. Time range is about 0 to 2 seconds.

This setting avoids excess acceleration, for example, from

16

0…(2

) - 1 x 512tCLK

VSTOP

to - VSTART

.

| Access Type   | Write, Read   |
|---------------|---------------|

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| XTARGET    | 31:0   | Target position for ramp mode. Write a new target position to this register to activate the ramp generator positioning in RAMPMODE = 0. Initialize all velocity, acceleration, and deceleration parameters before. Hint: The position is allowed to wrap around. Thus, XTARGET value optionally can be treated as an unsigned number. Note: The maximum possible displacement is ±((2 31 ) - 1). Hint: When increasing V1 , D1, or DMAX during a motion, rewrite XTARGET afterwards to trigger a second acceleration phase, if desired. -2 31 …+(2 31 ) - 1 |

| BITFIELD              |   BITS | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | DECODE                                                                                                             |
|-----------------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| hard_stop_clr_cur_int |     15 | Allows clearing the current PI integrator to prevent overcurrent on hard stop.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Do not clear integrator. 0x1: Clear integrator upon hard stop (recommended).                                  |
| virtual_stop_enc      |     14 | Source for virtual stop (VIRTUAL_STOP_L and VIRTUAL_STOP_R).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | 0x0: Virtual stop relates to ramp generator position XACTUAL. 0x1: Virtual stop relates to encoder position X_ENC. |
| en_virtual_stop_r     |     13 | Enables automatic motor stop during active right virtual stop condition.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                           |
| en_virtual_stop_l     |     12 | Enables automatic motor stop during active left virtual stop condition.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                           |
| en_softstop           |     11 | The soft stop mode always uses the deceleration ramp settings DMAX , V1 , D1 , V2 , D2 , VSTOP, and TZEROWAIT for stopping the motor. A stop occurs when the velocity sign matches the reference switch position (REFL, VIRTUAL_STOP_L for negative velocities, REFR, VIRTUAL_STOP_R for positive velocities, and the respective switch stop function is enabled. A hard stop also uses TZEROWAIT before the motor is released. Note: Do not use soft stop in combination with StallGuard2.                                                                                                                  | 0x0: Hard stop 0x1: Soft stop                                                                                      |
| sg_stop               |     10 | Enable stop by StallGuard(+). Disable to release motor after stop event. Program TCOOLTHRS for velocity threshold. This stop always results in a hard stop, ignoring the setting of en_softstop . Note: Do not enable during motor spin-up, wait until the motor velocity exceeds a certain value, where StallGuard(+) delivers a stable result. This velocity threshold should be programmed using TCOOLTHRS . Note: When used with STEP and DIR operation, an angle offset caused by the StealthChop+ angle regulator remains active until the motor is released by clearing the stall condition. This can | 0x0: disabled 0x1: enabled                                                                                         |
| en_latch_encoder      |      9 | Latch encoder position to ENC_LATCH upon reference switch event.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                           |
| latch_r_inactive      |      8 | Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an inactive going edge on the right reference                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                           |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD         |   BITS | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                  | DECODE                                                                                                                               |
|------------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                  |        | switch input REFR. The active level is defined by pol_stop_r .                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                      |
| latch_r_active   |      7 | Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an active going edge on the right reference switch input REFR. Note: Activate latch_r_active to detect any spurious stop event by reading status_latch_r . | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                                             |
| latch_l_inactive |      6 | Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an inactive going edge on the left reference switch input REFL. The active level is defined by pol_stop_l .                                                | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                                             |
| latch_l_active   |      5 | Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an active going edge on the left reference switch input REFL. Note: Activate latch_l_active to detect any spurious stop event by reading status_latch_l .  | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                                             |
| swap_lr          |      4 | Swap the left and right reference switch inputs REFL and REFR.                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Normal order 0x1: Left and right switch swapped                                                                                 |
| pol_stop_r       |      3 | Inverts the active polarity of the right reference switch input.                                                                                                                                             | 0x0: Noninverted, high active: a high level on REFR stops the motor. 0x1: Inverted, low active: a low level on REFR stops the motor. |
| pol_stop_l       |      2 | Inverts the active polarity of the left reference switch input.                                                                                                                                              | 0x0: Noninverted, high active: a high level on REFL stops the motor. 0x1: Inverted, low active: a low level on REFL stops the motor. |
| stop_r_enable    |      1 | Enables automatic motor stop during active right reference switch input. Hint: The motor restarts in case the stop switch is released.                                                                       | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                                             |
| stop_l_enable    |      0 | Enables automatic motor stop during active left reference switch input. Hint: The motor restarts in case the stop switch is released.                                                                        | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                                                                                             |

|-----------------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| status_virtual_stop_r |     15 | Virtual reference switch right status                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                     |

| status_sg             |     13 | Signals an active StallGuard2/StallGuard+ input from the driver. Hint: When polling this flag, stall events may be missed - activate sg_stop to ensure not to miss the stall event. | 0x0: Momentary inactive 0x1: Momentary active                 |
| second_move           |     12 | Signals that the automatic ramp required moving back in the opposite direction, example, due to on- the-fly parameter change.                                                       | 0x0: Normal motion 0x1: Direction is changed to reach target. |
| t_zerowait_active     |     11 | Signals that TZEROWAIT is active after a motor stop. During this time, the motor is in standstill.                                                                                  | 0x0: Not active 0x1: Motion controller in TZEROWAIT time.     |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD          |   BITS | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | DECODE                                                    |
|-------------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| vzero             |     10 | Signals that the actual velocity is 0.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | 0x0: Actual velocity is not 0. 0x1: Actual velocity is 0. |
| position_reached  |      9 | Signals that the target position is reached. This flag is set while XACTUAL and XTARGET match.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | 0x0: XTARGET not reached 0x1: XTARGET reached             |
| velocity_reached  |      8 | Signals that the target velocity is reached. This flag is set while VACTUAL and VMAX match.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 0x0: VMAX not reached 0x1: VMAX reached                   |
| event_pos_reached |      7 | Signals that the target position is reached ( position_reached is active). (interrupt condition).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                 |
| event_stop_sg     |      6 | Signals an active StallGuard2 stop event. Resetting the register clears the stall condition and the motor may restart motion, unless the motion controller is stopped. (interrupt condition)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                 |
| event_stop_r      |      5 | Active stop right condition due to stop switch or virtual stop. The stop condition and interrupt condition can be removed by setting RAMP_MODE to hold mode or by commanding a move to the opposite direction. In soft_stop mode, the condition remains active until the motor has stopped motion into the direction of the stop switch. Disabling the stop switch or stop function also clears the flag, but the motor continues motion. This bit is available for the interrupt output signal. | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                 |
| event_stop_l      |      4 | Active stop left condition due to stop switch or virtual stop. The stop condition and interrupt condition can be removed by setting RAMP_MODE to hold mode or by commanding a move to the opposite direction. In soft_stop mode, the condition remains active until the motor has stopped motion into the direction of the stop switch. Disabling the stop switch or the stop function also clears the flag, but the motor continues motion. This bit is available for the interrupt output.     | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                 |
| status_latch_r    |      3 | 1: Latch right ready (enable position latching using SW_MODE settings latch_r_active or latch_r_inactive ).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 0x0: Not latched 0x1: Latched                             |
| status_latch_l    |      2 | 1: Latch left ready (enable position latching using SW_MODE settings latch_l_active or latch_l_inactive ).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 0x0: Not latched 0x1: Latched                             |
| status_stop_r     |      1 | Reference switch right status                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active                                 |

## TMC5262

|---------------|--------|------------------------------|---------------------------|
| status_stop_l |      0 | Reference switch left status | 0x0: Inactive 0x1: Active |

| Reset         | -                    |                      |                      |                      |                      | 0b0                  | 0b0                  | 0b0                  |
| Access Type   | -                    | Write, Read          | Write, Read          | Write, Read          | Write, Read          | Write, Read          | Write, Read          | Write, Read          |
| BIT           | 7                    | 6                    | 5                    | 4                    | 3                    | 2                    | 1                    | 0                    |

| Reset         | 0b00                 | 0b00                 | 0b0                  | 0b0                  | 0b0                  | 0b0                  | 0b0                  | 0b0                  |

| BITFIELD        | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                             | DECODE                                                                                                |
|-----------------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| BEMF_FILTER_SEL | 29:28  | TriCoder filter corner frequency selection.             | 0x0: f CLK /32000 (0.5kHz) 0x1: f CLK /16000 (1kHz) 0x2: f CLK /7800 (2kHz) 0x3: f CLK /3700 (4.3kHz) |
| BEMF_BLANK_TIME | 23:16  | Blank time is BEMF_BLANK_TIME × 62,5ns ×( (2 14 ) - 1). |                                                                                                       |
| bemf_hyst       | 14:12  |                                                         | 0x0: 10mV 0x1: 25mV                                                                                   |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD        | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                 | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|-----------------|--------|---------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                 |        |                                             | 0x2: 50mV 0x3: 75mV 0x4: 100mV 0x5: 150mV 0x6: 200mV 0x7: 250mV                                                                                                                                                                              |
| nBEMF_ABN_SEL   | 11     | X_ENC source selector                       | 0x0: Back-EMF full step Tricoder 0x1: Incremental encoder interface                                                                                                                                                                          |
| enc_sel_decimal | 10     | Encoder prescaler mode selection            | 0x0: Encoder prescaler divisor binary mode: counts ENC_CONST(fractional part) /65536. 0x1: Encoder prescaler divisor decimal mode: counts in ENC_CONST(fractional part) /10000.                                                              |
| latch_x_act     | 9      | Position latch configuration                | 0x0: Disabled 0x1: Also latch XACTUAL position together with X_ENC . Allows latching the ramp generator position upon an N channel event as selected by pos_edge and neg_edge .                                                              |
| clr_enc_x       | 8      | Encoder latch configuration                 | 0x0: Upon N event, X_ENC is latched to ENC_LATCH only. 0x1: Latch and additionally clear encoder counter X_ENC at N-event.                                                                                                                   |
| pos_neg_edge    | 7:6    | N channel event sensitivity                 | 0x0: N channel event is active during an active N event level. 0x1: N channel is valid upon active going N event. 0x2: N channel is valid upon inactive going N event. 0x3: N channel is valid upon active going and inactive going N event. |
| clr_once        | 5      | Position latch configuration                | 0x0: Disabled 0x1: Latch or latch and clear X_ENC on the next N event following the write access.                                                                                                                                            |
| clr_cont        | 4      | Position latch configuration                | 0x0: Disabled 0x1: Always latch or latch and clear X_ENC upon an N event (once per revolution, it is recommended to combine this setting with edge sensitive N event).                                                                       |
| ignore_AB       | 3      | N event configuration                       | 0x0: An N event occurs only when polarities given by pol_N , pol_A and pol_B match. 0x1: Ignore A and B polarity for N channel event.                                                                                                        |
| pol_N           | 2      | Defines active polarity of N.               | 0x0: Low active 0x1: High active                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| pol_B           | 1      | Required B polarity for an N channel event. | 0x0: Negative polarity 0x1: Positive polarity                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| pol_A           | 0      | Required A polarity for an N channel event. | 0x0: Negative polarity 0x1: Positive polarity                                                                                                                                                                                                |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ENC_CONST  | 31:0   | Accumulation constant (signed) 16-bit integer part, 16-bit fractional part. X_ENC accumulates ± ENC_CONST /(2 16 × X_ENC ) (binary) or ± ENC_CONST /(10 4 x X_ENC ) (decimal) ENCMODE bit enc_sel_decimal switches between decimal and binary setting. Use the sign to match rotation direction! Binary: ± [µsteps/2 16 ] ±(0…32767.999847) Decimal: ±(0.0…32767.9999) Reset default = 1.0 (= 65536) |

| BITFIELD       | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|----------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| VIRTUAL_STOP_R | 31:0   | Virtual stop switch based on encoder. A stop is raised, based on the signed comparison. virtual_stop_enc = 1: X_ENC >= VIRTUAL_STOP_R virtual_stop_enc = 0: XACTUAL >= VIRTUAL_STOP_R -2 31 …+(2 31 ) - 1 |

## CUR\_ANGLE\_LIMIT (0x42)

| BIT                     | 31                              | 30                  | 29                                          | 28                | 27                | 26                 | 25                  | 24                |
|-------------------------|---------------------------------|---------------------|---------------------------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|--------------------|---------------------|-------------------|
| Field                   | cur_pi_neg_clip cur_pi_pos_clip | cur_pi_int_neg_clip | cur_pi_int_pos_clip                         |                   |                   | CUR_PI_LIMIT[11:8] |                     |                   |
| Reset                   |                                 |                     |                                             |                   | 0xFFF             | 0xFFF              | 0xFFF               | 0xFFF             |
| Access Type Read        | Only Read Only                  | Read Only           | Read Only                                   | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read        | Write, Read         | Write, Read       |
| BIT 23                  | 22                              | 21                  | 20                                          | 19                | 18                | 17                 |                     | 16                |
| CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]       | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]               | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]   | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]                           | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0] | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0] | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]  | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0]   | CUR_PI_LIMIT[7:0] |
| 0xFFF                   | 0xFFF                           | 0xFFF               | 0xFFF                                       | 0xFFF             | 0xFFF             | 0xFFF              | 0xFFF               | 0xFFF             |
| BIT 15                  | 14                              | 13                  | 12                                          |                   | 11                | 10                 | 9                   | 8                 |
| Field angle_pi_neg_clip | angle_pi_pos_clip               |                     | angle_pi_int_neg_clip angle_pi_int_pos_clip |                   | - -               |                    | ANGLE_PI_LIMIT[9:8] |                   |
| Reset                   |                                 |                     |                                             | -                 |                   |                    |                     |                   |
| Access Type Read Only   | Read Only                       |                     | Read Only                                   | Read Only         | - -               |                    | Write,              | Read              |
| BIT 7                   | 6                               | 5                   | 4                                           | 3                 | 2                 | 1                  |                     | 0                 |

| Field       | ANGLE_PI_LIMIT[7:0]   |
|-------------|-----------------------|
| Reset       | 0d256                 |
| Access Type | Write, Read           |

| BITFIELD              | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | DECODE                         |
|-----------------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|
| cur_pi_neg_clip       | 31     | Negative clipping of StealthChop+ current PI regulator to 0 (current too high).                                                                                                                                                  | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| cur_pi_pos_clip       | 30     | Positive clipping of StealthChop+ current PI regulator to CUR_PI_LIMIT (current not reached).                                                                                                                                    | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| cur_pi_int_neg_clip   | 29     | Negative clipping of StealthChop+ current PI regulator I-part to 0 (current too high).                                                                                                                                           | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| cur_pi_int_pos_clip   | 28     | Positive clipping of StealthChop+ current PI regulator I-part to CUR_PI_LIMIT (current not reached).                                                                                                                             | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| CUR_PI_LIMIT          | 27:16  | Unsigned limit for the output of the current regulator (limits PWM duty cycle by limiting PWM_CALC ). Set to 4095 for no limitation (typical setting).                                                                           |                                |
| angle_pi_neg_clip     | 15     | Negative clipping of angle PI regulator to - ANGLE_PI_LIMIT.                                                                                                                                                                     | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| angle_pi_pos_clip     | 14     | Positive clipping of angle PI regulator to ANGLE_PI_LIMIT.                                                                                                                                                                       | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| angle_pi_int_neg_clip | 13     | Negative clipping of angle PI regulator I-part to - ANGLE_PI_LIMIT.                                                                                                                                                              | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| angle_pi_int_pos_clip | 12     | Positive clipping of angle PI regulator I-part to ANGLE_PI_LIMIT.                                                                                                                                                                | 0x0: No clipping 0x1: Clipping |
| ANGLE_PI_LIMIT        | 9:0    | Unsigned limit for the output of the angle controller to ±ANGLE_PI_LIMIT (the default setting of 256 clips to +-90°, which is sufficient for normal operation and cannot cause overflow/underflow into the next current period). |                                |

| Field       | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         | ANGLE_ERROR[7:0]         |

| BIT         | 15                       | 14                       | 13                       | 12                       | 11                       | 10                       | 9                        | 8                        |
| Field       | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[9:8] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[9:8] |
| Reset       | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | 0x100                    | 0x100                    |
| Access Type | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        | -                        |                          |                          |
| BIT         | 7                        | 6                        | 5                        | 4                        | 3                        | 2                        | 1                        | 0                        |
| Field       | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] | ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT[7:0] |
| Reset       | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    | 0x100                    |
| Access Type | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              | Write, Read              |

| BITFIELD            | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|---------------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ANGLE_ERROR         | 25:16  | Actual error of current angle as calculated by MSCNT - ANGLE_MEAS.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT | 9:0    | Lower current limit for operation of angle regulator. If AMPL_MEAS <= ANGLE_LOWER_I_LIMIT, the angle regulator is disabled to avoid current measurement noise leading to regulation noise. Attention: Consider IRUN reduction, as selected by COOL_CUR_DIV , when setting a limit. CoolStep+ current reduction can lead to disabling the angle regulator when falling below this limit. This can compromise motor stability. |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| ANGLE_MEAS | 25:16  | (Signed) current angle calculated from ADC values (±256 = ±90°).                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| AMPL_MEAS  | 11:0   | Actual current amplitude calculated from ADC_I_A and ADC_I_B . This value is fed into the amplitude regulator. With a current scale of 250, the amplitude is regulated to a value of 2000. AMPL_MEAS covers a higher range to allow faster response of the PI regulator. |

## TMC5262

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| Access Type   | -                | -                 | -                 | -                 | -                 | -                 | Write, Read       | Write, Read       |
|---------------|------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| BIT           | 15               | 14                | 13                | 12                | 11                | 10                | 9                 | 8                 |
| Field         | -                | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] | COIL_INDUCT[14:8] |
| Reset         | -                |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |
| Access Type   | - Write, Read    | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     | - Write, Read     |
| BIT           | 7                | 6                 | 5                 | 4                 | 3                 | 2                 | 1                 | 0                 |
| Field         | COIL_INDUCT[7:0] | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  | COIL_INDUCT[7:0]  |
| Reset         |                  |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |                   |
| Access Type   | Write, Read      | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read       | Write, Read       |

| BITFIELD               | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | DECODE                                                                                  |
|------------------------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| rcoil_thermal_coupling | 17     | Enables thermal coupling for coil resistance measurement when in standstill. If one coil resistance is measurable (target current >50% of current setting and actually measured current >25% of current setting), the other coil resistance value is adapted according to the change of the measurable coil resistance if its actual current is below 50% of IRUN . | 0x0: No thermal coupling 0x1: Thermal coupling                                          |
| rcoil_manual           | 16     | Selection of source for coil resistance for StallGuard+ and CoolStep+.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 0x0: Automatic resistance measurement is used. 0x1: Resistance from user value is used. |
| COIL_INDUCT            | 14:0   | Enter motor inductance in [uH] for use with StallGuard+ and CoolStep+.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                         |

| Field       | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0] | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0] | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0] | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0] | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0]  | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0]  | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0]  | R_COIL_AUTO_B[7:0]  |
| Reset       |                    |                    |                    |                    |                     |                     |                     |                     |
| Access Type | Read Only          | Read Only          | Read Only          | Read Only          | Read Only           | Read Only           | Read Only           | Read Only           |

| BITFIELD      | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|---------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| R_COIL_AUTO_A | 27:16  | Measured coil resistance for coil A. It is measured when TSTEP > T_RCOIL_MEAS . The measurement is independent of the flag rcoil_manual . Coil resistance is updated only when the actual coil target current is above 50% of IRUN and measured current is above 25% of IRUN . |
| R_COIL_AUTO_B | 11:0   | Measured coil resistance for coil B. It is measured when TSTEP > T_RCOIL_MEAS . The measurement is independent of the flag rcoil_manual . Coil resistance is updated only when the actual coil target current is above 50% of IRUN and measured current is above 25% of IRUN . |

|--------------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
| SGP_LOW_VEL_CNTS   | 29:28  | Number of consecutive SGP_RESULT measurements with negative slope > SGP_LOW_VEL_SLOPE required for low velocity stall detection.                                                             | 0x0: 1 event 0x1: 2 events 0x2: 3 events 0x3: 4 events           |
| SGP_LOW_VEL_SLOPE  | 23:16  | Low velocity gradient of SGP_RESULT for stall detection.                                                                                                                                     |                                                                  |
| sgp_clear_cur_pi   | 14     | Allows clearing of the integral part of the current controller on stall detection in combination with hard stop. In SD_MODE, this flag also relates to hard stop initiated by external pins. | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                         |
| sgp_low_vel_freeze | 13     | Determines handling of SGP_RESULT in very low velocity ( TSTEP >TSGP_LOW_VEL_THRS). Freezing vs. Zeroing                                                                                     | 0x0: SGP_RESULT = 0 in very low velocity. 0x1: SGP_RESULT freeze |
| sgp_filt_en        | 12     | Enable SGP_RESULT filtering over one electrical period.                                                                                                                                      | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable                                         |
| SGP_THRS           | 8:0    | Stall threshold for StallGuard with SGP_RESULT- based stall                                                                                                                                  | detection.                                                       |

|--------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| load_filt_en | 4      | Enable RC-filter over 16 measurements on SG5_RAW value to filter coolstep input.                                                                    | 0x0: Disable 0x1: Enable (recommended)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| COOL_CUR_DIV | 3:0    | CoolStepPlus current reduction factor motor run current is ramped down to the programmed fraction of IRUN . Minimum current is IRUN / COOL_CUR_DIV. | 0x0: CoolStepPlus off 0x1: CoolStepPlus off, use PI_OFF_SPEED for current increment to full current. 0x2: Reduce to 50% of IRUN. 0x3: Reduce to 1/3 of IRUN. 0x4: Reduce to 1/4 of IRUN. 0x5: Reduce to 1/5 of IRUN. 0x6: Reduce to 1/6 of IRUN. 0x7: Reduce to 1/7 of IRUN. 0x8: Reduce to 1/8 of IRUN. 0x9: Reduce to 1/9 of IRUN. 0xA: Reduce to 1/10 of IRUN. 0xB: Reserved 0xC: Reserved 0xD: Reserved 0xE: Reserved 0xF: Reserved |

| BITFIELD                     | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------------------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| COOL_HI_GENERATORIC_RESERVE  | 31:24  | Load reserve used in generatoric mode when CoolStep current = IRUN . Ensure COOL_HI_GENERATORIC_RESERVE < COOL_LOW_GENERATORIC_RESERVE for best stability of regulation.             |
| COOL_LOW_GENERATORIC_RESERVE | 23:16  | Load reserve used in generatoric mode when CoolStep current is at minimum level. Ensure COOL_HI_GENERATORIC_RESERVE < COOL_LOW_GENERATORIC_RESERVE for best stability of regulation. |
| COOL_HI_LOAD_RESERVE         | 15:8   | Load reserve used when CoolStep current = IRUN . Ensure COOL_HI_LOAD_RESERVE < COOL_LOW_LOAD_RESERVE for best stability of regulation.                                               |
| COOL_LOW_LOAD_RESERVE        | 7:0    | Load reserve used at minimum CoolStep current. Ensure COOL_HI_LOAD_RESERVE < COOL_LOW_LOAD_RESERVE for best stability of regulation.                                                 |

| TMC5262   |
|-----------|

| BITFIELD              | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|-----------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| COOLSTEP_LOAD_RESERVE | 24:16  | Load reserve currently used by CoolStepPlus.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| SGP_RESULT            | 9:0    | SGP_RAW value filtered over the last or last four fullstep segments. Signed load value for StealthChop+ operation. A value near 0 corresponds to maximum motor load, a value from 200 to 255 means no or low motor load. Range 256 to 511 is generatoric operation, negative values occur upon overload, and typically indicate a step loss, respectively, danger of step loss. sgp_filt_en = 0: SGP_RAW values averaged over the last fullstep segment. sgp_filt_en = 1: SGP_RAW values averaged over the last four fullstep segments. |

|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| BIT           | 7             | 6             | 5             | 4             | 3             | 2             | 1             | 0             |
| Field         | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  | MSLUT_0[7:0]  |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| MSLUT_0    | 31:0   | Each bit gives the difference between entry x and entry x + 1 when combined with the corresponding MSLUTSELW bits: 0: W = %00: -1 %01: +0 %10: +1 %11: +2 1: W = %00: +0 %01: +1 %10: +2 %11: +3 This is the differential coding for the first quarter of a wave. Start values for CUR_A and CUR_B are stored for MSCNT position 0 in START_SIN and START_SIN90 . ofs31, ofs30, …, ofs01, ofs00 … ofs255, ofs254, …, ofs225, ofs224. Reset default = sine wave table |

| Access Type | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read |

BITFIELD

X3

BITS

31:24

DESCRIPTION

LUT segment 3 start.

The sine wave lookup table can be divided into up

to four segments using an individual step width

Wx

control entry

. The segment borders are selected

by X1

,

X2, and X3

.

Segment 0 goes from 0 to X1

Segment 1 goes from X1

to

Segment 2 goes from X2

-1.

X2

-1.

to X3

-1.

Segment 3 goes from X3

to 255.

For defined response, the values shall satisfy:

0&lt;

X1

&lt;

X2

&lt;

X3

DECODE

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| X2         | 23:16  | LUT segment 2 start. The sine wave lookup table can be divided into up to four segments using an individual step width control entry Wx . The segment borders are selected by X1 , X2, and X3 . Segment 0 goes from 0 to X1 -1. Segment 1 goes from X1 to X2 -1. Segment 2 goes from X2 to X3 -1. Segment 3 goes from X3 to 255. For defined response, the values shall satisfy: 0< X1 < X2 < X3 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| X1         | 15:8   | LUT segment 1 start. The sine wave lookup table can be divided into up to four segments using an individual step width control entry Wx . The segment borders are selected by X1 , X2, and X3 . Segment 0 goes from 0 to X1 -1. Segment 1 goes from X1 to X2 -1. Segment 2 goes from X2 to X3 -1. Segment 3 goes from X3 to 255. For defined response, the values shall satisfy: 0< X1 < X2 < X3 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| W3         | 7:6    | LUT width select from ofs ( X3 ) to ofs255 Width control bit coding W0 … W3 : %00: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: -1, +0 %01: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +0, +1 %10: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +1, +2 %11: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +2, +3                                                                                                                                                                | 0x0: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): -1 (+0) to sinewave. 0x1: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +0 (+1) to sinewave. 0x2: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +1 (+2) to sinewave. 0x3: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +2 (+3) to sinewave. |
| W2         | 5:4    | LUT width select from ofs ( X2 ) to ofs ( X3 -1) Width control bit coding W0 … W3 : %00: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: -1, +0 %01: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +0, +1 %10: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +1, +2 %11: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +2, +3                                                                                                                                                          | 0x0: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): -1 (+0) to sinewave. 0x1: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +0 (+1) to sinewave. 0x2: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +1 (+2) to sinewave. 0x3: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +2 (+3) to sinewave. |
| W1         | 3:2    | LUT width select from ofs ( X1 ) to ofs ( X2 -1) Width control bit coding W0 … W3 : %00: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: -1, +0 %01: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +0, +1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 0x0: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): -1 (+0) to sinewave. 0x1: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +0 (+1) to sinewave. 0x2: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +1 (+2) to sinewave. 0x3: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +2 (+3) to sinewave. |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| W0         | 1:0    | %10: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +1, +2 %11: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +2, +3 LUT width select from ofs00 to ofs ( X1 -1) Width control bit coding W0 … W3 : %00: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: -1, +0 %01: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +0, +1 %10: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +1, +2 %11: MSLUT entry 0, 1 select: +2, +3 | 0x0: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): -1 (+0) to sinewave. 0x1: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +0 (+1) to sinewave. 0x2: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +1 (+2) to sinewave. 0x3: Current MSLUT entry 0 (1): +2 (+3) to sinewave. |

## MSLUTSTART (0x69)

Start values are transferred to the microstep registers CUR\_A and CUR\_B whenever the reference position MSCNT = 0 is passed.

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| Access Type   | Write, Read   |
|---------------|---------------|

| BITFIELD     | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                   |
|--------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| OFFSET_SIN90 | 31:24  | Signed offset for cosine wave ±127 microsteps. Adapt START_SIN90 to match the microstep wave table at position MSCNT = 0.     |
| START_SIN90  | 23:16  | START_SIN90 gives the absolute value for cosine wave microstep table entry at MSCNT = 0 (table position 256 + OFFSET_SIN90 ). |
| START_SIN    | 7:0    | START_SIN gives the absolute value at microstep table entry 0.                                                                |

| Reset -       | -            | -            | -            | -            | -            | -            |              | -            |

| BIT 7         |              | 6            | 5            | 3            |              | 2            | 1            | 4 0          |
| Field         | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   | MSCNT[7:0]   |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| MSCNT      | 9:0    | Microstep counter. Indicates actual position in the microstep table for CUR_A . CUR_B uses an offset of 256 (two-phase motor). Note: Move to a position where MSCNT is zero before reinitializing MSLUTSTART or MSLUT and MSLUTSEL . |

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                           |
|------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| CUR_A      | 24:16  | Actual microstep current for motor phase A (co-sine wave) as read from MSLUT (not scaled by current). |

| BIT                     | 7                                | 6                                | 5                                | 4                                | 3           | 2           | 1                | 0           |
| Field                   | HEND_OFFSET[0] HSTRT_TFD210[2:0] | HEND_OFFSET[0] HSTRT_TFD210[2:0] | HEND_OFFSET[0] HSTRT_TFD210[2:0] | HEND_OFFSET[0] HSTRT_TFD210[2:0] | TOFF[3:0]   | TOFF[3:0]   | TOFF[3:0]        | TOFF[3:0]   |
