| BITFIELD   |   BITS | DESCRIPTION                     | DECODE                                                                                         |
|------------|--------|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| dedge      |     29 | Enable double edge step pulses. | 0x0: Disabled 0x1: Enable step impulse at each step edge to reduce step frequency requirement. |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD    | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|-------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| intpol      | 28     | Interpolation to 256 microsteps.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | 0x0: No interpolation 0x1: The actual microstep resolution ( MRES ) is extrapolated to 256 microsteps for smoothest motor operation (useful for STEP and DIR operation, only).                                                                            |
| MRES        | 27:24  | Microstep resolution selection. A reduced microstep resolution can be configured, especially for STEP and DIR operation. The resolution gives the number of microstep entries per sine quarter wave. The driver automatically uses microstep positions, which result in a symmetrical wave when choosing and switching to a lower microstep resolution. Step width = 2 MRES [microsteps] | 0x0: Native 256 microstep setting. Normally use this setting with the internal motion controller. 0x1: 128 0x2: 64 0x3: 32 0x4: 16 0x5: 8 0x6: 4 0x7: 2 0x8: FULLSTEP 0x9: FULLSTEP 0xA: FULLSTEP 0xB: FULLSTEP 0xC: FULLSTEP 0xD: FULLSTEP 0xE: FULLSTEP |
| TPFD        | 23:20  | Passive fast decay time. TPFD allows dampening of motor mid-range resonances. Passive fast decay time setting controls duration of the fast decay phase inserted after bridge polarity change. N CLK = 8μs × TPFD %0000: Disable %0001 …%1111: 1 …15                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| TBL         | 16:15  | Blank time selection for current measurement. Note: 1 or 2 is recommended for most applications.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | 0x0: ~1.25μs 0x1: ~1.75μs 0x2: ~2.5μs 0x3: ~3.625μs                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| chm         | 14     | Chopper mode                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 0x0: Standard mode (SpreadCycle) 0x1: Constant off-time with fast decay time. Fast decay time is also terminated when the negative nominal current is reached. Fast decay is after on-time.                                                               |
| disfdcc     | 12     | Fast decay mode. With chm = 1: disfdcc = 1 disables current comparator usage for termination of the fast decay cycle.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 0x0: Comparator terminates fast decay cycle 0x1: Fixed fast decay cycle                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| fd3         | 11     | TFD[3] With chm = 1: MSB of fast decay time setting                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | TFD.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| HEND_OFFSET | 10:7   | With chm = 0: HEND hysteresis low value. %0000 …%1111: Hysteresis is -3, -2, - 1, 0, 1, …, 12. (1/512 of this setting adds to current setting). This is the hysteresis value used for the hysteresis                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD     | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|--------------|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| HSTRT_TFD210 | 6:4    | With chm = 1: OFFSET sine wave offset. %0000 …%1111: Offset is -3, -2, - 1, 0, 1, …, 12. This is the sine wave offset and 1/512 of the value is added to the absolute value of each sine wave entry. With chm = 0: HSTRT hysteresis start value added to HEND. %000 …%111: Add 1, 2, …, 8 to hysteresis low value HEND. (1/512 of this setting adds to current setting). Attention: Effective HEND + HSTRT ≤ 16. Note: Hysteresis decrement is done each 16 clocks. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| TOFF         | 3:0    | TOFF off-time and driver enable Off-time setting controls duration of slow decay phase. N CLK = 24 + 32 x TOFF %0000: Driver disable, all bridges off. %0001: 1 - use only with TBL ≥ 2. %0010 …%1111: 2 …15                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | 0x0: Driver disabled and all bridges off. 0x1: Slow decay phase duration ~3.5 us 0x2: Slow decay phase duration ~5.5 us 0x3: Slow decay phase duration ~7.5 us 0x4: Slow decay phase duration ~9.5 us 0x5: Slow decay phase duration ~11.5 us 0x6: Slow decay phase duration ~13.5 us 0x7: Slow decay phase duration ~15.5 us 0x8: Slow decay phase duration ~17.5 us 0x9: Slow decay phase duration ~19.5 us 0xA: Slow decay phase duration ~21.5 us 0xB: Slow decay phase duration ~23.5 us 0xC: Slow decay phase duration ~25.5 us 0xD: Slow decay phase duration ~27.5 us 0xE: Slow decay phase duration ~29.5 us 0xF: Slow decay phase duration ~31.5 us |

| BIT         | 23           | 22          | 21          | 20          | 19          | 18          | 17          | 16          |
|-------------|--------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| Field       | thigh_sg_off | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    | sgt[6:0]    |
| Reset       |              | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   | 0b0000000   |
| Access Type | Write, Read  | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read |
| BIT         | 15           | 14          | 13          | 12          | 11          | 10          | 9           | 8           |
| Field       | seimin       | sedn[2:0]   | sedn[2:0]   | sedn[2:0]   | semax[3:0]  | semax[3:0]  | semax[3:0]  | semax[3:0]  |
| Reset       | 0b0          | 0b000       | 0b000       | 0b000       | 0x0         | 0x0         | 0x0         | 0x0         |
| Access Type | Write, Read  | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read |
| BIT         | 7            | 6           | 5           | 4           | 3           | 2           | 1           | 0           |
| Field       | -            | seup[1:0]   | seup[1:0]   | -           | semin[3:0]  | semin[3:0]  | semin[3:0]  | semin[3:0]  |
| Reset       | -            | 0b00        | 0b00        | -           | 0x0         | 0x0         | 0x0         | 0x0         |
| Access Type | -            | Write, Read | Write, Read | -           | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read | Write, Read |

| BITFIELD     | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|--------------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| sfilt        | 24     | StallGuard2 filter enable.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 0x0: Standard mode, high time resolution for StallGuard. 0x1: Filtered mode, StallGuard signal updated for each four fullsteps only to compensate for motor pole tolerances.                                                                                                                                          |
| thigh_sg_off | 23     | Stop-on-stall handling when exceeding VHIGH.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | 0x0: Exceeding VHIGH only disables CoolStep. 0x1: Exceeding VHIGH disables stop-on-stall function as well as CoolStep.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| sgt          | 22:16  | StallGuard2 threshold value. This signed value controls the StallGuard2 level for the stall signal output and sets the optimum measurement range for readout. A lower value gives a higher sensitivity. Zero is the starting value working with most motors. -64 to +63: a higher value makes StallGuard2 less sensitive and requires more torque to indicate a stall. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| seimin       | 15     | Minimum current for smart current control.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 0x0: 1/2 of current setting ( IRUN ). 0x1: 1/4 of current setting ( IRUN ).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| sedn         | 14:12  | Current down step speed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: For each 8 StallGuard2 values, decrease by one. 0x1: For each 4 StallGuard2 values, decrease by one. 0x2: For each 2 StallGuard2 values, decrease by one. 0x3: For each StallGuard2 value, decrease by one. 0x4: For each StallGuard2 value, decrease by two. 0x5: For each StallGuard2 value, decrease by four. |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD   | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                      | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|            |        |                                                                                                                                                                                  | 0x6: For each StallGuard2 value, decrease by eight. 0x7: For each StallGuard2 value, decrease by sixteen.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| semax      | 11:8   | StallGuard2 hysteresis value for smart current control. If the StallGuard2 result is equal to or above ( SEMIN + SEMAX + 1) × 32, the motor current is decreased to save energy. | 0x0: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 32) <= SG_RESULT. 0x1: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 64) <= SG_RESULT. 0x2: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 96) <= SG_RESULT. 0x3: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 128) <= SG_RESULT. 0x4: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 160) <= SG_RESULT. 0x5: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 192) <= SG_RESULT. 0x6: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 224) <= SG_RESULT. 0x7: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 256) <= SG_RESULT. 0x8: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 288) <= SG_RESULT. 0x9: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 320) <= SG_RESULT. 0xA: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 352) <= SG_RESULT. 0xB: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 384) <= SG_RESULT. 0xC: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 416) <= SG_RESULT. 0xD: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 448) <= SG_RESULT. 0xE: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 480) <= SG_RESULT. 0xF: Reduce current when (32 × SEMIN + 512) <= SG_RESULT. |
| seup       | 6:5    | Current up step width. Current increment steps per measured StallGuard2 value.                                                                                                   | 0x0: 8 0x1: 16 0x2: 32 0x3: 64                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| semin      | 3:0    | Minimum StallGuard2 value for smart current control and enable. If the StallGuard2 result falls below SEMIN × 32, the motor current is increased to reduce motor load angle.     | 0x1: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 32. 0x2: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 64. 0x3: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 96. 0x4: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 128. 0x5: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 160. 0x6: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 192. 0x7: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 224. 0x8: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 256. 0x9: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 288. 0xA: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 320. 0xB: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 352. 0xC: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 384. 0xD: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 416. 0xE: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 448. 0xF: Increase current on SG_RESULT < 480.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

| Access Type   | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      |
|---------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|----------------|
| BIT           | 23             | 22             | 21             | 20             | 19             | 18             | 17             | 16             |
| Field         | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] | CS_ACTUAL[7:0] |
| Reset         | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        | 0b00000        |
| Access Type   | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      |
| BIT           | 15             | 14             | 13             | 12             | 11             | 10             | 9              | 8              |
| Field         | -              | stealth        | s2vsb          | s2vsa          | ov             | seq_stopped    | SG_RESULT[9:8] | SG_RESULT[9:8] |
| Reset         | -              | 0b0            | 0b0            | 0b0            |                |                | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   |
| Access Type   | -              | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      |
| BIT           | 7              | 6              | 5              | 4              | 3              | 2              | 1              | 0              |
| Field         | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] | SG_RESULT[7:0] |
| Reset         | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   | 0b0000000000   |
| Access Type   | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      | Read Only      |

| BITFIELD   |   BITS | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|------------|--------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| stst       |     31 | Standstill indicator. This flag indicates motor standstill in each operation mode. Depending on fast_standstill setting, this occurs 2 20 clocks or 2 18 clocks after the last step pulse. | 0x0: Motor moving. 0x1: Motor in standstill.                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| olb        |     30 | Open load indicator phase B.                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Open load detected on phase B. Note: This is just an informative flag. The driver takes no action upon it. False detection may occur in fast motion and standstill. Check during slow motion only. |
| ola        |     29 | Open load indicator phase A.                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Open load detected on phase A. Hint: This is just an informative flag. The driver takes no action upon it. False detection may occur in fast motion and standstill. Check during slow motion only. |
| s2gb       |     28 | Short-to-ground indicator phase B.                                                                                                                                                         | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Short-to-GND detected on phase B. The driver is disabled. The flags stay active until the driver is disabled by software ( TOFF = 0) or by the ENN input.                                          |

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BITFIELD    | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                               |
|-------------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| s2ga        | 27     | Short-to-ground indicator phase A.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Short-to-GND detected on phase A. The driver is disabled. The flags stay active until the driver is disabled by software ( TOFF = 0) or by the ENN input. |
| otpw        | 26     | Overtemperature prewarning flag.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Overtemperature prewarning threshold is exceeded. The overtemperature prewarning flag is common for both bridges.                                         |
| ot          | 25     | Overtemperature flag.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Overtemperature limit is reached. Drivers are disabled until the IC has cooled down. The overtemperature flag is common for both bridges.                 |
| stallguard  | 24     | StallGuard2/StallGuard+ status.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Motor stall detected by StallGuard2 (in SpreadCycle operation), respectively, by StallGuard+ (in StealthChop+ operation).                                 |
| CS_ACTUAL   | 23:16  | Actual motor target current (actual CoolStep/CoolStep+ current of CoolStep function is enabled). Use for monitoring CoolStep and IRUN and IHOLD current scaling.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| stealth     | 14     | StealthChop+ indicator.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | 0x0: SpreadCycle mode 0x1: StealthChop+ mode                                                                                                                                         |
| s2vsb       | 13     | Short-to-supply indicator phase B.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: No error 0x1: Short-to-supply detected on phase B. The driver is disabled. The flags stay active until the driver is disabled by software ( TOFF = 0) or by the ENN input.      |
| s2vsa       | 12     | Short-to-supply indicator phase A.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | 0x0: No error 0x1: Short-to-supply detected on phase A. The driver is disabled. The flags stay active until the driver is disabled by software ( TOFF = 0) or by the ENN input.      |
| ov          | 11     | Overvoltage flag indicator. Flag is set if ADC_VSUPPLY >= OVERVOLTAGE_VTH.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | 0x0: Threshold not yet reached. 0x1: Threshold reached.                                                                                                                              |
| seq_stopped | 10     | 1: sequencer is stopped by ENC_A.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | 0x0: Sequencer running. 0x1: Sequencer stopped.                                                                                                                                      |
| SG_RESULT   | 9:0    | StallGuard2 result or StallGuard+ result (depending on chopper mode) or PWM on-time for coil A in standstill with SpreadCycle chopper for motor temperature estimation. Mechanical load measurement for SpreadCycle: StallGuard2 can measure mechanical motor load. A higher value means lower mechanical load. For StallGuard2, a value of 0 signals highest load. With optimum SGT setting, this is an indicator for a motor stall. The stall detection compares SG_RESULT to 0 to detect a stall. SG_RESULT is used as a base for CoolStep operation by comparing it to a pro- grammable upper limit and a lower limit. StallGuard2 works best with fine microstep operation. |                                                                                                                                                                                      |

| BITFIELD   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | DECODE   |
|------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
|            | In standstill, no StallGuard2 result can be obtained. SG_RESULT shows the chopper on-time for motor coil A instead. In combination with a sufficiently high-resistive motor, this measurement can be used to estimate motor coil temperature: move the motor to a determined microstep position at a certain current setting to get a rough estimation of motor temperature by reading the chopper on-time. As the motor heats up, its coil resistance rises and the chopper on-time increases. A more precise measurement of coil resistance, and with this estimation of motor temperature, is available with StalthChop+ In StallGuard+ (indicated by flag stealth ), SGP_RESULT is mirrored to SG_RESULT . In this mode, the range is 10-bit signed, rather than unsigned for StallGuard2. For StallGuard+ specifics, see SGP_RESULT . |          |

## TMC5262

## 65V/4ARMS+ Fully Integrated Smart Stepper Motor Controller and Driver

| BIT         | 7             | 6             | 5              | 4              | 3             | 2             | 1             | 0             |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|----------------|----------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|---------------|
| Field       | OL_THRSH[1:0] | OL_THRSH[1:0] | FREEWHEEL[1:0] | FREEWHEEL[1:0] | PWM_FREQ[3:0] | PWM_FREQ[3:0] | PWM_FREQ[3:0] | PWM_FREQ[3:0] |
| Reset       | 0x0           | 0x0           | 0b00           | 0b00           | 0x00          | 0x00          | 0x00          | 0x00          |
| Access Type | Write, Read   | Write, Read   | Write, Read    | Write, Read    | Write, Read   | Write, Read   | Write, Read   | Write, Read   |

| BITFIELD      | BITS   | DESCRIPTION                                                                                                                                                                                                       | DECODE                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|---------------|--------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| SD_ON_MEAS_HI | 19:16  | Higher hysteresis duty cycle threshold to switch ADC sample point from measurement during slow decay time to measurement during ON-time. SD_ON_MEAS_HI > SD_ON_MEAS_LO                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| SD_ON_MEAS_LO | 15:12  | Lower hysteresis duty cycle threshold to switch ADC sample point from measurement during ON time to measurement during slow-decay time. SD_ON_MEAS_HI > SD_ON_MEAS_LO                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| OL_THRSH      | 7:6    | Open load sensititvity for StealthChop+ operation (during motor rotation). Selects the minimum current value to be reached minimum one time within each half electrical rotation to avoid detection of open load. | 0x0: 0.125 x target current 0x1: 0.25 x target current 0x2: 0.5 x target current 0x3: 0.75 x target current                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| FREEWHEEL     | 5:4    | Selects StealthChop+ behaviour during standstill when motor current setting is zero (I_HOLD = 0).                                                                                                                 | 0x0: Normal operation 0x1: Freewheeling 0x2: Passive braking using LS drivers. 0x3: Passive braking using HS drivers.                                                                                                                                                                        |
| PWM_FREQ      | 3:0    | PWM frequency selection                                                                                                                                                                                           | 0x0: 19.5kHz 0x1: 24.4kHz 0x2: 29.3kHz 0x3: 34.2kHz 0x4: 39.1kHz 0x5: 44.0kHz 0x6: 48.8kHz 0x7: 53.7kHz 0x8: 58.6kHz 0x9: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xA: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xB: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xC: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xD: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xE: Reserved (58.6kHz) 0xF: Reserved (58.6kHz) |
